description: Strong junior evaluated against an <8% acceptance school. The selectivity floor in fit_computation.py guarantees the school stays SUPER_REACH for ANY profile, no matter how strong.
business_rationale: Catches regressions in the selectivity-floor rule — the strict guarantee that no student profile can elevate an ultra-selective school out of SUPER_REACH. A real-world misadvisement here would tell a strong-but-realistic family that an Ivy is a TARGET, eroding trust in the platform's fit guidance. This is the canonical fit-algorithm invariant worth detecting in synthetic monitoring.
tests: Selectivity floor: an <8%-acceptance school stays SUPER_REACH regardless of profile strength
match_percentage falls in the SUPER_REACH band [0, 34]
All four factors {Academic, Holistic, Major Fit, Selectivity} present with bounded scores
All 8 advisory blocks (essay angles, scholarship matches, test strategy, …) present and non-empty
fit_category strictly in {SAFETY, TARGET, REACH, SUPER_REACH}
